message.content:
You are an OPUS BUILDER executing ONE phase of Sol's authoritative build plan for POLARIS's fabrication-safety validator.

PLAN FILE (read it first): /home/polaris/wt/flywheel/sota_review/foundation/SOL_BUILD_PLAN.md
YOUR PHASE: P5 — "Positive machine-metadata salvage for unresolved manifestations"
ALREADY-LANDED PHASES (do not redo): P1, P1S, P2, P3, P4
REPO: /home/polaris/wt/flywheel

DO EXACTLY THIS:
1. Read /home/polaris/wt/flywheel/sota_review/foundation/SOL_BUILD_PLAN.md. Find the section for phase P5, and also read "## Global rules for every phase".
2. Implement the phase's EXACT edits in the files it names. Follow Sol's spec PRECISELY — do NOT invent mechanisms or substitute your own design. If the plan says "one reducer", unify to one; if it says "declarative table", use a table; there is NO rank scheme. 
3. Honor the GLOBAL RULES: generality (NO task-72 / no DOI, title, author, journal, or subject literals in any rule — rules fire by structural/typed signal only), positive-proof only (a gate may reject on absence but ADMIT only on positive evidence), and add the phase's required metamorphic/generality test.
4. Run the phase's ACCEPTANCE TEST exactly as the plan specifies. It MUST exit 0.
5. If it fails, FIX your implementation until the acceptance test passes — do NOT weaken or delete the test to make it pass. If you truly cannot make it pass, stop and report the blocker with the failing output.
6. Do NOT git commit. Do NOT work on other phases. Do NOT disturb outputs/ blobs, the ledger, or launch any long-running/LLM process.

SAFETY (paramount): this code decides whether 3.5M words of new corpus can be cited without fabrication. NEVER make a change that could let a DIFFERENT_WORK or UNRESOLVED_BINDING manifestation be attributed to a claimed source, or let a preprint/working-paper be cited as the journal. When uncertain, quarantine / fail closed. A prior session already hand-made partial "foundation" edits (identity gate in resolve_attribution, from_json re-derivation, evidence_miner ~line 1401 fixed to resolve_attribution(binding,policy), factored derive_binding_core, IDENTITY_PROVEN allowlist) — P1 hardens/verifies these; later phases build on them.

Return the schema: implemented (bool), acceptance_passed (did the test exit 0), the exact test_command, test_output_tail (~last 20 lines), files_changed, a one-paragraph summary, and blocker (empty string if none).

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	# Binding-gate build plan
2	
3	I reviewed the authoritative design note and the live tree. The six hand edits are directionally correct, but they are not yet sufficient to ship. The remaining critical issues are:
4	
5	- Two independent version derivations still disagree.
6	- Generic call paths still default to `JOURNAL_ONLY`.
7	- Prompt-derived source policy can be lost in fallback contract paths.
8	- Correspondence still trusts the old whole-body `identity.verdict`.
9	- Unresolved/different works still reach the expensive mining lane.
10	- Machine-readable self-metadata is not yet used for positive salvage.
11	- The composer and argument planner still contain live task-specific defaults.
12	
13	The workflow should execute the eight gated phases below in order: P1, P1S, P2–P7. P1S is the explicit source-policy phase required by the operator; numbering P2–P7 remains aligned with the requested phase names.
14	
15	## Global rules for every phase
16	
17	Every phase must preserve these invariants:
18	
19	1. No DOI, title, author, venue, journal, topic, benchmark ID, or subject string may participate in a production decision.
20	2. Decisions may depend only on typed work metadata, artifact structure, self-identifying metadata, byte-derived observations, verified span relationships, and the original prompt.
21	3. The identity gate is always active and is independent of source policy.
22	4. Missing, malformed, stale, or unknown values fail closed.
23	5. `JOURNAL_ONLY` is selected only from a positive constraint in the original prompt. Otherwise use `ANY_VERSION`.
24	6. Each phase must add at least one metamorphic test where identifiers and subject vocabulary are replaced while structure is held constant.
25	7. Do not tune production rules to make the 501-row cohort count come out correctly. Cohort counts are regression observations, not rule inputs.
26	8. Do not advance to the next phase until the phase command exits zero.
27	
28	---
29	
30	## P1 — Finish and harden the six foundation edits
31	
32	### Files
33	
34	- [scripts/event_ledger.py](/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/event_ledger.py)
35	- [scripts/provenance.py](/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/provenance.py)
36	- [scripts/evidence_miner.py](/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/evidence_miner.py)
37	- [scripts/cellcog_composer.py](/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/cellcog_composer.py)
38	- [scripts/argument_planner.py](/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/argument_planner.py)
39	- [scripts/synthesis_contract.py](/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/synthesis_contract.py)
40	- New: `tests/test_binding_gate_foundation.py`
41	- New: `tests/test_runtime_contract_generality.py`
42	
43	### Exact edits
44	
45	#### 1. `derive_binding_core` and `IDENTITY_PROVEN`
46	
47	Keep both additions in `event_ledger.py`.
48	
49	Correct the string comparison:
50	
51	```python
52	if binding is SAME_WORK:
53	```
54	
55	must become:
56	
57	```python
58	if binding == SAME_WORK:
59	```
60	
61	Keep `derive_semantic_binding(events)` as a thin wrapper. No other identity reducer may be introduced.
62	
63	All verdict consumers must import or call the same `IDENTITY_PROVEN`; do not reproduce its members in another tuple.
64	
65	#### 2. Identity derivation in `ingest_bytes()`
66	
67	Keep the live `observe_text(text) → derive_binding_core(...)` path and the stored:
68	
69	```python
70	profile["semantic_binding"]
71	profile["semantic_binding_basis"]
72	profile["semantic_binding_derived_by"]
73	```
74	
75	Keep `DIFFERENT_WORK` on the quarantine-expression path. It must:
76	
77	- create no claimed-expression edge;
78	- use `kind="unknown"`;
79	- use an expression ID structurally derived from work ID and disposition;
80	- retain the manifestation bytes.
81	
82	Do not copy `row["semantic_binding"]` into each manifestation.
83	
84	In `migrate()`, compare a row-level cached verdict only to the row’s selected `fulltext_manifestation`, when that relationship is explicitly present. Store the comparison as audit-only data:
85	
86	```python
87	profile["row_binding_cache"] = {
88	    "stored": ...,
89	    "live": ...,
90	    "agrees": bool,
91	}
92	```
93	
94	No resolver or constructor may read this field to grant permission.
95	
96	#### 3. `Attribution` fields
97	
98	Keep:
99	
100	```python
101	identity_verdict
102	disposition
103	reason_code
104	```
105	
106	Define stable constants for dispositions and reason codes in `provenance.py`; do not scatter string literals through callers.
107	
108	Stamp every resolver return, including invalid bindings:
109	
110	- invalid span: `QUARANTINE / SPAN_BINDING_INVALID`;
111	- different work: `QUARANTINE / IDENTITY_DIFFERENT_WORK`;
112	- unresolved: `LEAD_ONLY / IDENTITY_UNRESOLVED`;
113	- missing/unknown verdict: `QUARANTINE / IDENTITY_UNKNOWN_VERDICT`;
114	- incomplete: `QUARANTINE / INCOMPLETE_BYTES`;
115	- policy mismatch: `LEAD_ONLY / VERSION_NOT_PERMITTED`;
116	- successful resolution: `ADMIT / ADMITTED`.
117	
118	Every refusal must have:
119	
120	```python
121	admitted is False
122	names_expression_id is None
123	text is None
124	permitted_expression_ids == ()
125	```
126	
127	for identity/integrity failures.
128	
129	#### 4. Resolver identity gate
130	
131	Keep its location after span verification and before completeness and expression policy.
132	
133	Remove the unused local imports of each version constant. Import only the canonical allowlist and the two explicit negative verdicts.
134	
135	Do not compute a permissive fallback from the legacy `profile["identity"]["verdict"]`.
136	
137	#### 5. Strict JSON load
138	
139	Keep live re-derivation and refusal on disagreement, but tighten it:
140	
141	- Missing stored `semantic_binding` is an integrity error, not an old-file compatibility path.
142	- Unknown stored verdict is an integrity error.
143	- Re-derive both verdict and basis.
144	- Use the live verdict and live basis after validation.
145	- A stored row-cache verdict is never used.
146	- Update `_take()` to recognize dataclass defaults mechanically with `dataclasses.MISSING`; do not maintain a special handwritten list such as `{"profile", "kind"}`.
147	
148	Migration is the compatibility path for legacy corpus rows. Strict graph load must not silently migrate an old graph.
149	
150	#### 6. Miner binding call
151	
152	Keep the corrected call:
153	
154	```python
155	graph.resolve_attribution(binding, policy)
156	```
157	
158	Add a regression proving that exact-span correspondence is visible only when the binding, rather than the bare manifestation ID, is passed.
159	
160	#### Remove live task-specific runtime defaults
161	
162	The current downstream runtime still contains task-specific production values.
163	
164	In `cellcog_composer.py`:
165	
166	- Delete the fixed AI/labor `WRITE_PROMPT` subject.
167	- Delete the fixed `OUTLINE`.
168	- Delete the fixed report title.
169	- Change writer input to accept the compiled `research_contract.Contract`, its question/title, and `research_contract.derive_outline(...)`.
170	- Render source-method prose from `Contract.source_policy.compliance_prose()`.
171	- Card instructions must say “permitted source” or the derived policy description, not always “peer-reviewed journal article.”
172	- `abstract_nodes()` and `methods_nodes()` must receive the contract and derive their wording from it.
173	
174	In `argument_planner.py`:
175	
176	- Remove the production `default_contract()` containing AI/labor facets.
177	- Replace it with `contract_from_research_contract(contract, outline)`.
178	- A no-contract call produces an empty, facet-agnostic contract, not an AI contract.
179	- Any AI fixture belongs under tests or an explicitly named demo helper used only by `__main__`.
180	
181	In `synthesis_contract.py`:
182	
183	- The historical task-specific members have already been removed from `SAFE_CAPS`; preserve that.
184	- Delete the `SAFE_CAPS` authority entirely from production validation.
185	- Capitalized names are permitted only when present in a premise, in the original question/compiled contract, sentence-initial by orthography, or emitted from a typed renderer enum.
186	- Replace fixed `LEVEL_CUES` as an authority with per-contract facet matchers or span receipts. Fixed social-science vocabulary must not decide clinical, legal, or CS evidence.
187	- `argument_planner.owned_is_safe()` must use the same structural predicate, not import a second allowlist.
188	
189	Historical examples may remain in comments and tests, but no runtime default may contain or select the task subject.
190	
191	### Acceptance test
192	
193	Create foundation tests covering:
194	
195	- live foreign DOI derives `DIFFERENT_WORK`;
196	- changing only requested DOI makes the same bytes reject under both policies;
197	- changing row cache from `DIFFERENT_WORK` to `SAME_WORK` does not change live resolution;
198	- missing and unknown verdicts fail closed;
199	- invalid binding is structurally stamped;
200	- JSON verdict tampering is refused;
201	- the corrected `gate_card()` passes its binding.
202	
203	Generality test:
204	
205	- compile and render one clinical, one legal, and one CS prompt;
206	- none may inherit AI, labor-market, journal-only, or 4IR wording;
207	- a capitalized entity absent from premises/contract is rejected;
208	- replacing it with a completely different entity produces the same structural result;
209	- putting that entity into a premise makes it admissible without adding it to an allowlist.
210	
211	Gate:
212	
213	```bash
214	cd /home/polaris/wt/flywheel
215	PYTHONPATH=scripts python -m pytest -q \
216	  tests/test_binding_gate_foundation.py \
217	  tests/test_runtime_contract_generality.py
218	PYTHONPATH=scripts python scripts/provenance.py
219	```
220	
221	### Depends on
222	
223	None.
224	
225	---
226	
227	## P1S — Infer the adjustable source policy from the original prompt
228	
229	### Files
230	
231	- [scripts/research_contract.py](/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/research_contract.py)
232	- [scripts/evidence_miner.py](/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/evidence_miner.py)
233	- [scripts/provenance.py](/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/provenance.py)
234	- [scripts/event_ledger.py](/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/event_ledger.py)
235	- New: `tests/test_source_policy_derivation.py`
236	
237	### Exact prompt-derivation rule
238	
239	Add a deterministic, clause-scoped function in `research_contract.py`:
240	
241	```python
242	derive_version_scope(question: str) -> tuple[str, list[str]]
243	```
244	
245	It returns `("JOURNAL_ONLY", evidence_clauses)` only when a clause positively directs the system to use published, peer-reviewed, or journal sources.
246	
247	A positive directive requires both:
248	
249	1. A source-class phrase:
250	
251	```text
252	journal article/source/publication/literature
253	peer-reviewed article/source/publication/literature
254	published article/study/source/paper
255	```
256	
257	2. A directive or exclusivity relation in the same clause:
258	
259	```text
260	use, cite, include, draw on, rely on, restrict to, limit to,
261	must be, should be, ensure sources are, only, exclusively, solely
262	```
263	
264	Negation or widening in the same clause defeats `JOURNAL_ONLY`, including:
265	
266	```text
267	not limited to journals
268	do not restrict to journals
269	include preprints/working papers/accepted manuscripts
270	journal articles and preprints
271	```
272	
273	Mere discussion of journals, publication, peer review, or comparisons between source types does not set the gate.
274	
275	No source-class demand means `ANY_VERSION`.
276	
277	The function must return the exact verbatim clause(s) that justified the restriction. The LLM contract compiler may not invent, remove, or broaden this value.
278	
279	### Wiring
280	
281	In `research_contract.SourcePolicy`, add:
282	
283	```python
284	version_scope: str = "ANY_VERSION"
285	version_scope_evidence: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
286	```
287	
288	`_floor_source_policy(question)` sets these exclusively through `derive_version_scope(question)`.
289	
290	In `evidence_miner.load_contract()`:
291	
292	- Every return path—explicit facets, compiled contract, cache, question-term fallback, and no-question fallback—must pass through one finalizer.
293	- The finalizer derives provenance policy from the original `question`, not from the most recently cached contract.
294	- `JOURNAL_ONLY` maps to `provenance.JOURNAL_ONLY`.
295	- `ANY_VERSION` maps to `provenance.ANY_VERSION`.
296	- Remove the current inference from `peer_reviewed_only + excluded_types`.
297	- Remove the `PEER_REVIEWED` intermediate behavior from this two-state version policy.
298	
299	Correct generic defaults:
300	
301	```python
302	Graph.resolve_attribution(..., source_policy=ANY_VERSION)
303	gate_card(...): ... or P.ANY_VERSION
304	MiningContract.source_policy: ANY_VERSION
305	```
306	
307	Keep `journal_attributable()` explicit about passing `JOURNAL_ONLY`.
308	
309	In `mine()`:
310	
311	- The contract-derived policy is authoritative when `question` is nonempty.
312	- An explicit `source_policy` that disagrees with a nonempty prompt-derived policy raises `ValueError`.
313	- Explicit policy remains allowed for low-level tests with `question == ""`.
314	
315	In `event_ledger.py`:
316	
317	- Replace `journal_articles_only: bool = True` in `derive_eligibility()` and `record_eligibility()`.
318	- Accept an explicit `SourcePolicy`, defaulting to `ANY_VERSION`.
319	- Version eligibility must consult the policy’s permitted expression kinds.
320	- A preprint is not unconditionally a discovery lead under `ANY_VERSION`.
321	- Remove task-number language from function semantics.
322	
323	### Acceptance test
324	
325	The same valid, complete preprint fixture must produce:
326	
327	| Prompt | Policy | Result |
328	|---|---|---|
329	| “Summarize the evidence about X.” | `ANY_VERSION` | `ADMIT`, names preprint |
330	| “Use journal articles and preprints about X.” | `ANY_VERSION` | `ADMIT`, names preprint |
331	| “Explain differences between journals and preprint servers.” | `ANY_VERSION` | `ADMIT`, names preprint |
332	| “Use only peer-reviewed sources about X.” | `JOURNAL_ONLY` | `LEAD_ONLY` |
333	| “Cite published journal articles about X.” | `JOURNAL_ONLY` | `LEAD_ONLY` |
334	| “Do not limit this to journal articles; include working papers.” | `ANY_VERSION` | `ADMIT` |
335	
336	For `JOURNAL_ONLY`, assert:
337	
338	```python
339	admitted is False
340	disposition == "LEAD_ONLY"
341	reason_code == "VERSION_NOT_PERMITTED"
342	names_expression_id is None
343	```
344	
345	Metamorphic test: replace X with unrelated clinical, legal, economics, and CS subjects; policy must not change.
346	
347	Gate:
348	
349	```bash
350	cd /home/polaris/wt/flywheel
351	PYTHONPATH=scripts python -m pytest -q tests/test_source_policy_derivation.py
352	```
353	
354	### Depends on
355	
356	P1.
357	
358	---
359	
360	## P2 — Unify version derivation and quarantine impossible pairs
361	
362	### Design decision
363	
364	Use one version reducer. Do not retain two competing derivations and choose between them later.
365	
366	`version_evidence()` becomes the single reducer that returns both:
367	
368	```python
369	VersionDecision(
370	    semantic_binding,
371	    expression_kind,
372	    evidence_key,
373	    basis,
374	)
375	```
376	
377	`ingest_bytes()` must assign its expression kind from this result. `derive_binding_core()` must use the same result when identity is proven.
378	
379	The resolver still validates the resulting pair, but that is an invariant check against tampering or in-memory corruption—not a second version derivation.
380	
381	### Files
382	
383	- [scripts/event_ledger.py](/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/event_ledger.py)
384	- [scripts/provenance.py](/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/provenance.py)
385	- New: `tests/test_version_derivation_unification.py`
386	
387	### Exact edits
388	
389	In `event_ledger.py`:
390	
391	- Replace `VERSION_EVIDENCE` triples with typed ordered rules that specify both semantic binding and expression kind.
392	- Split working-paper and preprint observations structurally.
393	- Preserve precedence:
394	
395	```text
396	accepted manuscript
397	working paper
398	preprint
399	published/proceedings version
400	no version evidence
401	```
402	
403	- Cover-sheet and article-front voices remain separately recorded.
404	- Accepted/preprint/working evidence vetoes published evidence.
405	- Repository names may be registry data, but no DOI, title, author, journal, or subject may appear in the reducer.
406	- For non-scholarly typed works, derive `official_text` or `registry_record` from `Work.kind`, not scholarly furniture.
407	
408	In `provenance.py`:
409	
410	- `profile()` may retain artifact diagnostics, but it must not independently decide the expression kind.
411	- Make `derive_expression_kind()` a compatibility wrapper over the shared observations and `version_evidence()`, or remove it after all callers migrate.
412	- In `ingest_bytes()`, obtain `VersionDecision` once and use its `expression_kind` and basis.
413	- Store the decision’s evidence key in the profile.
414	- In `Graph.from_json()`, re-run the same reducer and refuse disagreement with:
415	  - stored expression kind;
416	  - the manifestation’s expression node kind;
417	  - stored semantic version when identity is proven.
418	- In `resolve_attribution()`, after identity and before completeness, validate the pair against one declarative compatibility table.
419	- Any incompatible pair returns:
420	
421	```python
422	admitted=False
423	disposition="QUARANTINE"
424	reason_code="DERIVATION_CONFLICT"
425	names_expression_id=None
426	text=None
427	permitted_expression_ids=()
428	```
429	
430	### Exact pair decision table
431	
432	Assume valid span, identity-proven bytes, and completeness. Correspondence exceptions apply only to the exact bound span.
433	
434	| Semantic binding | Own expression kind | Pair status | `JOURNAL_ONLY` | `ANY_VERSION` |
435	|---|---|---|---|---|
436	| `VERSION_OF_PUBLISHED` | `journal_version` | consistent | ADMIT journal | ADMIT journal |
437	| `VERSION_OF_PUBLISHED` | `proceedings_version` | consistent | LEAD_ONLY | ADMIT proceedings |
438	| `VERSION_OF_ACCEPTED` | `accepted_manuscript` | consistent | LEAD_ONLY; exact verified journal correspondence may admit that span | ADMIT accepted manuscript |
439	| `VERSION_OF_PREPRINT` | `working_paper` | consistent | LEAD_ONLY; exact verified journal correspondence may admit that span | ADMIT working paper |
440	| `VERSION_OF_PREPRINT` | `preprint` | consistent | LEAD_ONLY; exact verified journal correspondence may admit that span | ADMIT preprint |
441	| `SAME_WORK` | `official_text` for typed case/statute | consistent | LEAD_ONLY | ADMIT official text |
442	| `SAME_WORK` | `registry_record` for typed trial/registry work | consistent | LEAD_ONLY | ADMIT registry record |
443	| `SAME_WORK` | `unknown` | unresolved version, not conflict | LEAD_ONLY | LEAD_ONLY |
444	| Any proven semantic version | Any other own expression kind | conflict | QUARANTINE | QUARANTINE |
445	
446	In particular:
447	
448	```text
449	VERSION_OF_PREPRINT + journal_version = DERIVATION_CONFLICT
450	VERSION_OF_ACCEPTED + journal_version = DERIVATION_CONFLICT
451	VERSION_OF_PUBLISHED + working_paper/preprint/accepted_manuscript = DERIVATION_CONFLICT
452	```
453	
454	There is no rank scheme and no “choose the safer-looking label.”
455	
456	### Metamorphic acceptance vector
457	
458	Create one accepted fixture, then mutate only structural version furniture:
459	
460	1. Published furniture → `VERSION_OF_PUBLISHED / journal_version`.
461	2. Add accepted-manuscript stamp → both outputs change to accepted.
462	3. Replace accepted stamp with working-paper stamp → both outputs change to preprint/working.
463	4. Relabel the graph expression node to `journal_version` without changing bytes → loader refuses and resolver returns `DERIVATION_CONFLICT`.
464	5. Repeat with unrelated clinical, legal, and CS titles/authors; results remain identical.
465	
466	The real seven known conflicts must become zero admissible conflicts without listing their identifiers.
467

631	### Exact data model
632	
633	Add an explicit `IdentityReceipt` dataclass containing at least:
634	
635	```python

655	For field-based PDF metadata, offsets are offsets inside the named metadata field. For XMP/HTML/JATS, offsets are byte or decoded-character offsets in the raw artifact. The location type must make the coordinate space explicit.

667	
668	`migrate()` matches the selected `fulltext_manifestation` or exact `text_sha256` against `row["manifestations"]` and passes its raw blob reference. It must not copy a receipt or verdict from the row.
669	
670	### Exact extraction rule
671	
672	Run `reresolve_unresolved_metadata(graph, blob_store)` only after baseline manifestations exist, and only over live `UNRESOLVED_BINDING`.
673	
674	Admissible self-fields are:
675	
676	#### PDF, through PyMuPDF/fitz
677	
678	- DOI: PDF Info or XMP self-metadata fields.
679	- Title: PDF Info/XMP title.
680	- Authors: PDF Info/XMP author/creator.
681	
682	Do not inspect rendered body or reference text.
683	
684	#### HTML
685	
686	Inside `<head>` only:
687	
688	- identifier: `citation_doi`, `DC.identifier`;
689	- title: `citation_title`, `DC.title`;
690	- author: repeated `citation_author`, `DC.creator`.
691	
692	#### JATS
693	
694	Inside `<front><article-meta>` only:
695	
696	- identifier: `<article-id pub-id-type="doi">`;
697	- title: `<article-title>`;
698	- authors: `<contrib contrib-type="author">`, using structured name fields.
699	
700	Never search `<body>` or `<back>`.
701	
702	### Positive promotion rule
703	
704	Normalize DOI by parsing DOI URI/prefix forms, case-folding, percent-decoding, and stripping terminal citation punctuation.
705	
706	Normalize title with Unicode NFKC, entity decoding, case-folding, punctuation-to-space, and whitespace collapse. Title match is exact after normalization—no fuzzy threshold.
707	
708	Normalize authors structurally:
709	
710	- compare requested family name to a structured surname field; or
711	- compare an exact normalized full-name sequence;
712	- a single requested surname must equal a metadata surname token, not be a substring.
713	
714	Promote only when one of these holds:
715	
716	1. Exact requested DOI in a permitted self-identifier field.
717	2. Exact normalized requested title and at least one requested author in permitted self-identity fields.
718	
719	Conflict rule:
720	
721	- if self-identifier fields contain both target and foreign DOI values, remain unresolved with `IDENTITY_METADATA_CONFLICT`;
722	- title/author evidence cannot override a conflicting self-identifier;
723	- absence of metadata changes nothing.
724	
725	A verified receipt becomes an observation consumed by `derive_binding_core()`. The reducer then re-derives `SAME_WORK` or the appropriate version verdict. Do not directly assign a promoted verdict in the salvage loop.
726	
727	### Loader revalidation
728	
729	`Graph.from_json(..., blob_store=...)` must:
730	
731	1. load raw bytes by immutable blob ID;
732	2. verify raw hash;
733	3. rerun the named metadata extractor/version;
734	4. re-find the exact raw match at the recorded location;
735	5. re-normalize the value;
736	6. re-evaluate it against the Work;
737	7. compare the canonical live receipt to the stored receipt;
738	8. re-run `derive_binding_core()` using verified receipts;
739	9. refuse the graph on any mismatch or unavailable receipt artifact.
740	
741	A graph with receipts cannot load without access to the referenced blob store.
742	
743	### OCR status

745	Do not implement OCR.
746

752	effect: residual glyph-header manifestations remain LEAD_ONLY

754

551	    end = hits[-1].end()

561	    `provenance.derive_expression_kind` has always used, so the two lanes cannot diverge:
562	
563	      * am / wp / preprint stamps are read from the whole head (a library describing the file it
564	        deposited is testimony about these bytes — the cover sheet MAY CONVICT);
565	      * journal furniture is read ONLY from the article's own body, with the cover sheet segmented off
566	        (a cover sheet's DOI / citation block is the library CITING the article of record, which is the
567	        one thing the article of record itself never has to do — the cover sheet MAY NOT ACQUIT).
568	
569	    `version_evidence()` maps these booleans to a VersionDecision; this function decides nothing.
570	    """
571	    head = text[:12000]
572	    _cover, document = _segment_cover_sheet(head)
573	    return {
574	        'am':       bool(_ACCEPTED_STAMP.search(head)),
575	        'wp':       bool(_WP_MARK.search(head)),
576	        'preprint': bool(_PREPRINT_MARK.search(head)),
577	        'journal':  bool(_JOURNAL_MARK.search(document[:12000])),
578	        'has_cover_sheet': bool(_cover),

582	def observe_text(text: str, header_chars: int = 1500) -> dict:
583	    """Everything the ledger needs to know about a blob of retrieved text — and NOT ONE CONCLUSION.
584	
585	    Note `header_chars`: a preprint stamp is only evidence of what a document IS when it appears in
586	    the document's OWN header. Every economics paper cites NBER working papers in its REFERENCES —
587	    scanning the whole text for "NBER Working Paper" identifies the bibliography, not the paper.
588	    (That naive scan called the actual Journal of Economic Perspectives article a working paper.)
589	    """
590	    words = text.split()
591	    header = text[:header_chars]
592	    # THE ARTICLE'S OWN FRONT MATTER, with any repository cover sheet segmented off it. The DOIs found
593	    # HERE are the document's own claim about which article it is — as distinct from the DOI a library
594	    # printed on a cover page, which is the library's claim about what it filed.
595	    cover, article_front = segment_front_matter(text, header_chars)
596	    front_dois = sorted({d.rstrip('.').lower() for d in _DOI_RE.findall(article_front)})
597	    cid_tokens = sum(1 for t in words if 'cid:' in t)
598	    stripped = _CID.sub('', text)
599	    real_words = len(re.findall(r'[A-Za-z]{3,}', stripped))
600	    # How much REAL text is in the header? A title page of pure (cid:NN) glyph codes decodes to
601	    # nothing — and a header we cannot READ is a header we cannot CHECK AN IDENTITY AGAINST. It is
602	    # not a stranger's paper. It is an unreadable one, and those are different facts.
603	    header_real = len(re.findall(r'[A-Za-z]{3,}', _CID.sub('', header)))
604	    return {
605	        'word_count': len(words),
606	        'char_count': len(text),
607	        'readable_word_count': real_words,
608	        'glyph_garbage_ratio': round(cid_tokens / max(1, len(words)), 4),
609	        'chrome_markers': len(_CHROME.findall(text)),
610	        # ---- VERSION FURNITURE, READ IN THE RIGHT VOICE ----------------------------------------

613	        # WHAT IT FILED. These were previously read from the same undifferentiated `header` slice, so a

616	        'has_cover_sheet': bool(cover),
617	        'published_stamp_in_header': bool(_PUBLISHED_STAMP.search(article_front)),

619	        'accepted_stamp_in_header': bool(_ACCEPTED_STAMP.search(article_front)),
620	        'accepted_stamp_in_cover': bool(_ACCEPTED_STAMP.search(cover)),
621	        'preprint_stamp_in_header': bool(_PREPRINT_STAMP.search(article_front)),
622	        'preprint_stamp_in_cover': bool(_PREPRINT_STAMP.search(cover)),

624	        'header_real_words': header_real,

630	        'header_text': header[:400],

657	NOT_INDEXED          = 'NOT_INDEXED_BY_THIS_BACKEND'  # 404 — a fact about THEIR INDEX, not the world
658	BACKEND_FAILED       = 'BACKEND_FAILED'               # 429/503/timeout — a fact about OUR REQUEST

663	#: The four kinds that constitute A REQUEST. CANDIDATE_IDENTIFIED and MANIFESTATION_FETCHED also carry
664	#: an `adapter` (for provenance — WHICH backend found this), but they are not request outcomes and must
665	#: not be read as one.

669	#: Worst-first. An adapter that hung on one request and answered another has NOT answered: we asked it

1113	# ---- 4. semantic binding ---------------------------------------------------------------------
1114	
1115	SAME_WORK        = 'SAME_WORK'              # this IS the journal article
1116	VERSION_PUBLISHED = 'VERSION_OF_PUBLISHED'  # a repository copy OF the published version
1117	VERSION_ACCEPTED = 'VERSION_OF_ACCEPTED'    # an ACCEPTED MANUSCRIPT. Peer review is not the last edit.
1118	VERSION_PREPRINT = 'VERSION_OF_PREPRINT'    # a working paper. A DIFFERENT SOURCE until proven equal.
1119	DIFFERENT_WORK   = 'DIFFERENT_WORK'         # we fetched somebody else's paper
1120	UNRESOLVED       = 'UNRESOLVED_BINDING'
1121	
1122	#: THE IDENTITY ALLOWLIST (Sol, binding-gate design §1). A span may be ATTRIBUTED only when the bytes'
1123	#: identity is POSITIVELY PROVEN to be the requested Work. This is an ALLOWLIST, not a denylist: a
1124	#: missing, malformed, stale, or newly-introduced verdict is NOT in it and therefore REJECTS by default.
1125	#: DIFFERENT_WORK -> quarantine (a stranger's paper); UNRESOLVED_BINDING -> lead-only (we cannot tell);
1126	#: both are `admitted=False`. Version disposition (which EXPRESSION, under which policy) is orthogonal
1127	#: and decided downstream — identity only says "these bytes ARE this Work".
1128	IDENTITY_PROVEN = frozenset({SAME_WORK, VERSION_PUBLISHED, VERSION_ACCEPTED, VERSION_PREPRINT})
1129	
1130	
1131	#: ══ WHICH EXPRESSION DO THESE BYTES TESTIFY TO BEING, AND WHAT SEMANTIC VERSION DOES THAT MAKE THEM? ═
1132	#:
1133	#: THE ONE VERSION REDUCER (Sol P2). It returns BOTH the semantic binding and the own-expression kind
1134	#: from ONE pass over ONE set of observations, so `ingest_bytes` (which needs the expression kind) and
1135	#: `derive_binding_core` (which needs the semantic binding) can never again be TWO derivations that
1136	#: disagree. There is exactly one mapping from expression kind to semantic binding, below, and the
1137	#: resolver's compatibility table is literally its inverse — so the pair the reducer emits is, by
1138	#: construction, always a consistent pair, and the ONLY way an inconsistent pair can reach the resolver
1139	#: is tampering or in-memory corruption, which is what DERIVATION_CONFLICT quarantines.
1140	#:
1141	#: THE ORDER OF THE RULES IS THE PRECEDENCE, AND IT IS A STATEMENT — not an accident of where an `if`
1142	#: landed. Accepted / working-paper / preprint furniture VETOES published furniture, because the
1143	#: commonest shape in an institutional repository carries BOTH at once:
1144	#:
1145	#:     "This is an author produced version of a paper published in <journal>.     <- ACCEPTED
1146	#:      Citation for the published version: ..."                                  <- PUBLISHED
1147	#:
1148	#: Reading the SECOND line and ignoring the FIRST is how an accepted manuscript became journal evidence.
1149	#: The precedence lives HERE, in data (accepted, then working paper, then preprint, then published, then
1150	#: no evidence), and `derive_eligibility` / the resolver re-check the result against tables that do not
1151	#: consult this order at all.
1152	
1153	@dataclass(frozen=True)
1154	class VersionDecision:
1155	    """The ONE reducer's output: BOTH the semantic binding AND the own-expression kind, plus the
1156	    evidence key that fired and a human basis. `ingest_bytes` assigns its expression kind from this;
1157	    `derive_binding_core` uses its semantic binding when identity is proven; the two therefore agree
1158	    by construction, never by coincidence."""
1159	    semantic_binding: str
1160	    expression_kind: str
1161	    evidence_key: str
1162	    basis: str
1163	
1164	
1165	#: THE SINGLE MAPPING expression-kind -> semantic-binding. The resolver's COMPATIBLE_VERSION_PAIRS is
1166	#: built as its inverse (below), so the reducer and the gate cannot drift: adding a version kind here
1167	#: without deciding its binding is impossible, because both come from this one dict.
1168	_EXPRESSION_TO_BINDING: dict[str, str] = {
1169	    'journal_version':     VERSION_PUBLISHED,
1170	    'proceedings_version': VERSION_PUBLISHED,
1171	    'accepted_manuscript': VERSION_ACCEPTED,
1172	    'working_paper':       VERSION_PREPRINT,
1173	    'preprint':            VERSION_PREPRINT,
1174	    'official_text':       SAME_WORK,
1175	    'registry_record':     SAME_WORK,
1176	    'unknown':             SAME_WORK,
1177	}
1178	
1179	#: THE ONE DECLARATIVE COMPATIBILITY TABLE (Sol P2). The resolver validates the pair
1180	#: (semantic_binding, own_expression_kind) against THIS and nothing else. It is the inverse of the
1181	#: reducer's own mapping, so a pair the reducer can emit is always in it, and a pair it cannot emit
1182	#: (e.g. VERSION_OF_PREPRINT + journal_version) is a DERIVATION_CONFLICT — the medRxiv/working-paper-
1183	#: as-journal P0, quarantined structurally.
1184	COMPATIBLE_VERSION_PAIRS: frozenset[tuple[str, str]] = frozenset(
1185	    (binding, kind) for kind, binding in _EXPRESSION_TO_BINDING.items())
1186	
1187	#: Typed ordered rules — (evidence-furniture key, own expression kind, basis). The order IS the
1188	#: precedence. Working-paper and preprint furniture are SPLIT structurally (two rules, two expression
1189	#: kinds), though both map to VERSION_OF_PREPRINT. No DOI, title, author, journal or subject appears
1190	#: here — only the structural furniture booleans version_furniture() observed.
1191	VERSION_EVIDENCE: tuple[tuple[str, str, str], ...] = (
1192	    ('am',       'accepted_manuscript',
1193	     'the bytes (or the repository that filed them) declare this an ACCEPTED MANUSCRIPT — acceptance '
1194	     'precedes copy-editing, proofs and the editor\'s last round, and THE NUMBERS MOVE ACROSS THEM'),
1195	    ('wp',       'working_paper',
1196	     'the header carries a working-paper stamp — peer review CHANGES NUMBERS; this is a DIFFERENT '
1197	     'SOURCE until version equivalence is proven'),
1198	    ('preprint', 'preprint',
1199	     'the header carries a preprint stamp — peer review CHANGES NUMBERS; this is a DIFFERENT SOURCE '
1200	     'until version equivalence is proven'),
1201	    ('journal',  'journal_version',
1202	     'typeset journal furniture in the article\'s OWN front matter (not a cover sheet), and NO '
1203	     'accepted-manuscript or working-paper or preprint stamp contradicting it'),
1204	)
1205

1207	def version_evidence(prof: dict, work_kind: str = 'study') -> VersionDecision:
1208	    """THE ONE PLACE version furniture becomes a (semantic binding, expression kind) pair.
1209	
1210	    For a non-scholarly TYPED work (case / statute / trial) the version taxonomy is not the scholarly
1211	    one — there is no preprint of a statute — so the expression is derived from `Work.kind` via the
1212	    registry, never from scholarly furniture the opinion happens to quote. For a scholarly work the
1213	    typed ordered rules (VERSION_EVIDENCE) decide, in precedence order.
1214	    """
1215	    fam = _WORK_KIND_ARTIFACT.get(work_kind or 'study')
1216	    if fam is not None:
1217	        ek = KIND_PROFILE[fam]['expression']    # official_text / registry_record
1218	        return VersionDecision(
1219	            _EXPRESSION_TO_BINDING[ek], ek, f'work_kind:{work_kind}',
1220	            f'work kind `{work_kind}` — the version taxonomy of a {fam.replace("_", " ")} is not the '
1221	            f'scholarly one; these bytes are its {ek.replace("_", " ")}')
1222	    furn = prof.get('version_furniture') or {}
1223	    for key, ek, why in VERSION_EVIDENCE:
1224	        if furn.get(key):
1225	            return VersionDecision(_EXPRESSION_TO_BINDING[ek], ek, key, why)
1226	    return VersionDecision(SAME_WORK, 'unknown', 'none',
1227	                           'no self-identifying version furniture — the version is UNRESOLVED, though '
1228	                           'the identity is not')

1231	def _norm(s: str) -> set[str]:
1232	    stop = {'the', 'a', 'an', 'of', 'and', 'for', 'in', 'on', 'to', 'from', 'with'}
1233	    return {w for w in re.findall(r'[a-z]+', (s or '').lower()) if w not in stop and len(w) > 2}

1236	def derive_semantic_binding(events: list[Event]) -> tuple[str, dict]:
1237	    """Is the text we fetched the work we asked for? A fetcher MAY NOT ANSWER THIS ABOUT ITSELF.
1238	
1239	    Sol: "A predecessor working paper and later journal article may be closely related, but they are
1240	    DIFFERENT SOURCES until version equivalence is proven."
1241	    """
1242	    # THE SAME DOCUMENT THE LABEL DESCRIBES. `_best_holding` returns a manifestation TOGETHER WITH ITS
1243	    # OWN profile — so identity is checked against the header of the very bytes whose completeness was
1244	    # judged. Reading "the last manifestation" and "the last profile" independently could pair one
1245	    # document's byline with another document's word count, and answer about a document that does not
1246	    # exist.
1247	    best = _best_holding(events)
1248	    if best is None:
1249	        return UNRESOLVED, {'reason': 'nothing was fetched'}
1250	    fetched, prof, _cls, _info = best
1251	    return derive_binding_core(
1252	        requested_doi=fetched.get('requested_doi', ''),
1253	        requested_title=fetched.get('requested_title', ''),
1254	        requested_authors=fetched.get('requested_authors', []),
1255	        prof=prof,
1256	        document_title=fetched.get('document_title', ''),
1257	        observed_byline=fetched.get('byline', ''),
1258	        work_kind=fetched.get('work_kind', 'study'))

1260	
1261	def derive_binding_core(*, requested_doi: str, requested_title: str,
1262	                        requested_authors: list, prof: dict,
1263	                        document_title: str = '', observed_byline: str = '',
1264	                        work_kind: str = 'study') -> tuple[str, dict]:
1265	    """THE single per-manifestation identity rule. `derive_semantic_binding(events)` is a thin wrapper
1266	    that pulls the requested identity + profile off the ledger and calls this; `provenance.ingest_bytes`
1267	    calls it directly with the Work's identity and `observe_text(bytes)`. ONE reducer, both paths — a
1268	    second identity rule in the graph is exactly the split that let DIFFERENT_WORK bytes be admitted.
1269	
1270	    `prof` is an `observe_text()` result (or a stored profile with the same keys): it supplies
1271	    front_matter_dois, byline_present, header_real_words, identity_window/header_text, and the version
1272	    stamps. `requested_*` is what we ASKED for (the Work). Positive evidence only — DIFFERENT_WORK
1273	    needs a foreign DOI or a disjoint byline; absence is never a stranger's paper."""
1274	    # The identity window is BOUNDED (see observe_text): a whole-document scan finds every author in
1275	    # the references and "confirms" anything you ask it to.
1276	    window = document_title or prof.get('identity_window', '') \
1277	        or prof.get('header_text', '')
1278	    want_t = _norm(requested_title)
1279	    got_t = _norm(window)
1280	    want_a = {a.lower() for a in (requested_authors or [])}
1281	    byline = (observed_byline or window).lower()
1282	
1283	    overlap = len(want_t & got_t) / max(1, len(want_t))
1284	    author_hit = any(a in byline for a in want_a if a)
1285	    title_match = overlap >= 0.90
1286	    # A SPECIFIC title (>=4 content words) matching in full is itself strong identity evidence.
1287	    # A GENERIC one is not: {rise, machines} sits entirely inside {mathematics, rise, machines}.
1288	    title_specific = len(want_t) >= 4
1289	
1290	    # ── THE DOCUMENT'S OWN DOI (Sol V9 §5) ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
1291	    # Read from the ARTICLE'S front matter, with any repository cover sheet segmented off. This is the
1292	    # ONE identity signal that is both positive and decisive in BOTH directions: an exact front-matter
1293	    # DOI CONFIRMS, and a front matter that prints a DIFFERENT DOI and NOT ours is the positive evidence
1294	    # of a stranger's paper that DIFFERENT_WORK has always needed and never had.
1295	    want_doi = str(requested_doi or '').strip().lower().rstrip('.')
1296	    front_dois = [str(d).lower().rstrip('.') for d in (prof.get('front_matter_dois') or [])]
1297	    doi_hit = bool(want_doi and want_doi in front_dois)
1298	    foreign_doi = bool(want_doi and front_dois and not doi_hit)
1299	    #: A byline was POSITIVELY OBSERVED. Absence of one is NOT disjointness — see _BYLINE.
1300	    byline_present = bool(prof.get('byline_present'))
1301	
1302	    ev = {'title_overlap': round(overlap, 2), 'author_in_byline': author_hit,
1303	          'title_content_words': len(want_t),
1304	          'requested_title': (requested_title or '')[:70],
1305	          'front_matter_dois': front_dois[:4], 'doi_in_front_matter': doi_hit,
1306	          'cover_sheet_chars': prof.get('cover_sheet_chars', 0),
1307	          'header_text': (prof.get('header_text') or '')[:120]}
1308	
1309	    # ── AN UNREADABLE HEADER IS NOT A STRANGER'S PAPER. ───────────────────────────────────────
1310	    # Autor, Levy & Murnane (2003) arrives as a PDF whose title page is pure (cid:NN) glyph codes:
1311	    # ZERO readable words in the header, though the BODY has 10,902. An earlier version of this
1312	    # reducer read "no title, no author" as DIFFERENT_WORK — turning a FONT-ENCODING FAILURE into a
1313	    # confident claim about whose paper it is. That is this project's whole disease, committed by
1314	    # the cure. We cannot read it, therefore we cannot bind it. Say exactly that.
1315	    if prof.get('header_real_words', 0) < 10:
1316	        return UNRESOLVED, {'reason': f'the header decoded to {prof.get("header_real_words", 0)} '
1317	                                      f'readable words — we CANNOT READ it, so we cannot establish '
1318	                                      f'identity. Not a stranger\'s paper: an unreadable one', **ev}
1319	
1320	    # ── DIFFERENT_WORK NEEDS POSITIVE EVIDENCE OF A STRANGER. IT ALWAYS DID. ──────────────────
1321	    # Sol V9 §5 sets the bar exactly: DIFFERENT_WORK requires "a positive foreign DOI, or an
1322	    # incompatible foreign title PLUS a disjoint byline". And, verbatim: "A generic title without an
1323	    # author must NOT produce DIFFERENT_WORK; the current event reducer is TOO AGGRESSIVE here."
1324	    #
1325	    # It was. Two branches below used to return DIFFERENT_WORK on ABSENCE:
1326	    #
1327	    #   1. a generic-title match with no author in the header -> DIFFERENT_WORK ("title collision")
1328	    #   2. an author match with a weak title                  -> DIFFERENT_WORK ("same author, different
1329	    #                                                                             paper")
1330	    #
1331	    # Neither is positive evidence of anyone else's paper. (1) fires on a real article with a two-word
1332	    # title whose PDF header happens not to carry a byline — which describes a large fraction of
1333	    # publisher HTML. (2) fires on the RIGHT paper whose title page decoded badly, and it fires while the
1334	    # BYLINE AGREES WITH US, which is the opposite of a disjoint byline. Both convict on absence, and
1335	    # DIFFERENT_WORK is not a shrug — it is `wrong_work`, a QUARANTINE, and it destroys the document.
1336	    #
1337	    # UNRESOLVED is the honest verdict for both, and it costs us the same evidence while making a claim
1338	    # we can actually support. Absence of our evidence read as evidence of absence is the 429-means-"no
1339	    # free copy exists" error, and it does not become sound by being pointed at a PDF.
1340	    if foreign_doi:
1341	        # POSITIVE. The document PRINTS A DOI ON ITSELF, and it is not ours. (Cover sheet already
1342	        # segmented off — a library's citation of us is not the document identifying itself as us.)
1343	        return DIFFERENT_WORK, {
1344	            'reason': f'the article front matter prints DOI {front_dois[0]!r}, and we asked for '
1345	                      f'{want_doi!r}. The document says whose it is, and it is not ours', **ev}
1346	
1347	    # ── IDENTITY IS CONFIRMED BY ITS OWN DOI, AN AUTHOR, OR A SPECIFIC TITLE IN FULL. ─────────
1348	    confirmed = doi_hit or author_hit or (title_match and title_specific)
1349	
1350	    if not confirmed:
1351	        # POSITIVE, the second way: A DISJOINT BYLINE. The document SAYS WHO WROTE IT, we know who we
1352	        # asked for, and they are different people — while nothing else (its DOI, its title) puts our
1353	        # work in these bytes. That is testimony from the document, not an inference from silence.
1354	        #
1355	        # BOTH halves are required, and both are checked:
1356	        #   - `byline_present`: an authorship cue was POSITIVELY OBSERVED in the article's own front
1357	        #     matter (cover sheet segmented off). An ABSENT byline is not a disjoint one.
1358	        #   - `want_a`: we actually asked for named authors. If we asked for none, no byline on earth
1359	        #     can be disjoint from them, and claiming otherwise convicts every document we hold.
1360	        #
1361	        # THIS IS THE PARRY / YANG-HUI HE CASE, and it stays quarantined: we asked for Parry, the front
1362	        # matter says "By Yang-Hui He", and the only thing that "matched" was the two-word phrase "rise
1363	        # of the machines". What convicts it is the BYLINE — not the title collision, which convicts
1364	        # nothing.
1365	        if byline_present and want_a and not author_hit:
1366	            return DIFFERENT_WORK, {
1367	                'reason': f'the article front matter carries a byline, and it names NONE of the '
1368	                          f'{len(want_a)} requested author(s). Nothing else identifies these bytes as '
1369	                          f'ours (title overlap {overlap:.0%}, no matching front-matter DOI) — an '
1370	                          f'incompatible work with a DISJOINT BYLINE', **ev}
1371	        if title_match and not title_specific:
1372	            # A TITLE COLLISION, with NO byline to convict on. {rise, machines} sits inside
1373	            # {mathematics, rise, machines}. Sol V9 §5, verbatim: "A generic title without an author must
1374	            # NOT produce DIFFERENT_WORK." This is not knowledge that we hold a stranger's paper. It is
1375	            # knowledge that WE CANNOT TELL — and those are different facts, one of which is true.
1376	            return UNRESOLVED, {
1377	                'reason': f'the requested title has only {len(want_t)} content word(s) and no requested '
1378	                          f'author appears in the front matter. A {overlap:.0%} match on a GENERIC title '
1379	                          f'is a TITLE COLLISION — it establishes NEITHER identity NOR its absence. '
1380	                          f'UNRESOLVED, and therefore not attributable', **ev}
1381	        return UNRESOLVED, {'reason': f'title overlap {overlap:.0%}, no requested author in the '
1382	                                      f'header — identity is NOT established, so this text may not '
1383	                                      f'be attributed to this source', **ev}
1384	
1385	    # ...an author alone is not identity either: prolific authors write many papers. But the byline
1386	    # AGREES WITH US, so this is NOT a stranger's paper — it is a paper we cannot pin down. This branch
1387	    # used to return DIFFERENT_WORK ("same author, DIFFERENT PAPER") and it was the second half of Sol's
1388	    # "too aggressive" finding: it convicted while the byline was CONFIRMING us, which is the exact
1389	    # opposite of the disjoint byline the rule requires.
1390	    if author_hit and not doi_hit and not title_match and not title_specific:
1391	        return UNRESOLVED, {'reason': f'a requested author appears in the front matter but the title '
1392	                                      f'matches only {overlap:.0%} — same author, possibly a different '
1393	                                      f'paper. The byline is NOT disjoint, so this is not positive '
1394	                                      f'evidence of a stranger\'s work: it is UNRESOLVED', **ev}
1395	
1396	    # IDENTITY IS SETTLED — these bytes ARE the work we asked for. The remaining question is WHICH
1397	    # EXPRESSION of it, and that is decided in ONE place, by the ONE version reducer (version_evidence),
1398	    # which returns BOTH the semantic binding and the own-expression kind so that this lane and the
1399	    # expression lane cannot diverge. No reordering of this function can promote a manuscript to the
1400	    # version of record: the precedence lives in VERSION_EVIDENCE, in data.
1401	    dec = version_evidence(prof, work_kind)
1402	    ev = {**ev, 'expression_kind': dec.expression_kind, 'version_evidence_key': dec.evidence_key}
1403	    if dec.semantic_binding == SAME_WORK:
1404	        return SAME_WORK, {'reason': 'identity confirmed by '
1405	                                     + ('the article\'s OWN front-matter DOI' if doi_hit

214	EDGE_TYPES = ('exact_copy_of', 'accepted_manuscript_of', 'predecessor_of',
215	              'reports_same_study', 'supersedes', 'cites')
216	
217	# THE ONLY EXPRESSION-WIDE EDGE THAT MAY WIDEN AN ATTRIBUTION.
218	# A span in manifestation M may name expression E != M.expression ONLY across this edge, and ONLY when
219	# the edge is ASSERTED. `predecessor_of` and `reports_same_study` are deliberately absent: a working
220	# paper and the journal article report the same study, and PEER REVIEW CHANGES NUMBERS.
221	#
222	# `accepted_manuscript_of` WAS IN THIS TUPLE AND IS NOT ANY MORE. (Sol, V9 §4: "`accepted_manuscript_of`
223	# remains a useful bibliographic edge, but it is not span-preserving.") It stayed here on the theory that
224	# an accepted manuscript is post-peer-review and therefore says what the journal says. It is not, and it
225	# does not: copy-editing, proof corrections, and the editor's own last round all land AFTER acceptance,
226	# and the numbers move. Worse, the edge was reachable FROM REPOSITORY METADATA — version_align mapped
227	# Unpaywall's `acceptedVersion` string straight onto it and alignment_census declared the result
228	# journal-ADMISSIBLE. That is a live fabrication path: a repository's one-word label, three hops later,
229	# printing a manuscript's number under a journal's name.
230	#
231	# AN ACCEPTED MANUSCRIPT IS NEVER THE JOURNAL VERSION MERELY BECAUSE A REPOSITORY SAYS `acceptedVersion`.
232	# What CAN carry a span from an accepted manuscript into the journal is a verified SpanCorrespondence —
233	# THAT SPAN, those two hashes, those two offsets, exact canonical equality — and nothing else.
234	SPAN_PRESERVING = ('exact_copy_of',)

236	#: An edge that is a real bibliographic fact and transfers NO attribution whatsoever. Kept explicit so

238	NON_SPAN_PRESERVING = tuple(t for t in ('exact_copy_of', 'accepted_manuscript_of', 'predecessor_of',

244	# 1b. PER-SPAN ATTRIBUTION — THE SpanCorrespondence  (Sol V9 §4)
245	# =================================================================================================
246	"""
247	THE API MOVED FROM MANIFESTATION-WIDE PERMISSION TO BINDING-SPECIFIC PERMISSION.
248	
249	The old shape was `resolve_attribution(manifestation_id, policy)`: ONE answer for a whole document. An
250	edge that widened it widened it for EVERY span in the manifestation at once — so proving that ONE
251	sentence of an accepted manuscript survived into the journal would have licensed the OTHER four hundred,
252	including the ones peer review rewrote. That is permission by association, and the association is exactly
253	what we cannot check.
254	
255	A `SpanCorrespondence` is the narrowest true thing: THIS span, in THESE bytes, is character-for-character
256	THAT span, in THOSE bytes. It carries both manifestation ids, both content hashes, both offset pairs,
257	both verbatim spans, the canonicalization ALGORITHM AND VERSION under which they were compared, the exact
258	canonical-span hash, and the semantic-binding verdict for each manifestation. The verifier RECHECKS all of it —
259	the hashes, the offsets, and exact canonical text equality — because a correspondence that arrives from a
260	JSON file is an untrusted input, like everything else here.
261	
262	Consequences, all of them mechanical below:
263	
264	  * Repository metadata can NEVER prove span equivalence. There is no field on this record where a
265	    repository's opinion could be written; the record is made of hashes and offsets or it is not made.
266	  * A correspondence grants permission FOR THAT SPAN ONLY — not for every assertion in the manuscript.
267	  * "0.37 versus 0.2" FAILS IMMEDIATELY: canonicalization does not touch digits, so the canonical texts
268	    differ and `verify_correspondence()` returns False before any policy is consulted.
269	  * A span found independently in the VoR's own bytes should be REBOUND to the VoR manifestation
270	    (`Graph.rebind()`), which is strictly stronger than a correspondence: no cross-document permission
271	    is needed at all when the span is IN the document you want to name.
272	  * An accepted manuscript with no VoR bytes stays accepted-manuscript-attributable ONLY.
273	"""
274	
275	#: THE CANONICALIZATION. Named and VERSIONED, because two documents compared under different rules were
276	#: never compared. It does exactly two things — Unicode compatibility normalisation (so a ligature or a
277	#: non-breaking space in a PDF is not a "difference"), and whitespace collapse (so a column break is not
278	#: a difference either).
279	#:
280	#: WHAT IT DELIBERATELY DOES NOT DO, and why each omission is load-bearing:
281	#:   - it does not case-fold      : "Table 3" and "table 3" may genuinely be different objects
282	#:   - it does not strip punctuation: "0.37" vs "037" is not a rounding, it is a different number
283	#:   - it does not normalise digits or units: THIS IS THE ACEMOGLU TEST. 0.37pp in the NBER working
284	#:     paper, 0.2pp in the published JPE. Any "smart" numeric tolerance here would align them, and the
285	#:     whole point of this file is that PEER REVIEW CHANGES NUMBERS.
286	CANON_ALGORITHM = 'nfkc_whitespace_collapse'
287	CANON_VERSION = '1'
288	
289	
290	def canonicalize(s: str) -> str:
291	    """The ONE canonicalization. Its name and version travel with every correspondence that uses it."""
292	    return re.sub(r'\s+', ' ', unicodedata.normalize('NFKC', s or '')).strip()
293	
294	
295	def canonical_hash(s: str) -> str:
296	    """sha256 of the canonical form, DOMAIN-SEPARATED BY ALGORITHM AND VERSION.
297	
298	    Without the prefix, a hash computed under algorithm v1 and a hash computed under a future v2 would be
299	    indistinguishable strings, and a stored hash would silently certify a comparison that was never made.
300	    """
301	    return hashlib.sha256(
302	        f'{CANON_ALGORITHM}:{CANON_VERSION}:{canonicalize(s)}'.encode('utf-8', 'ignore')).hexdigest()
303	
304	
305	def binding_id(manifestation_id: str, content_hash: str, start: int, end: int) -> str:
306	    """The identity of a BOUND SPAN — the unit `resolve_attribution()` now answers about.
307	
308	    It is derived, not minted: the same span of the same bytes always has the same id, and a binding whose
309	    offsets or whose source bytes were edited IS A DIFFERENT BINDING and cannot inherit the old one's
310	    permission.
311	    """
312	    h = hashlib.sha256(f'{manifestation_id}|{content_hash}|{start}|{end}'.encode()).hexdigest()
313	    return f'bind:{h[:16]}'

317	class SpanCorrespondence:
318	    """THIS span in THESE bytes IS THAT span in THOSE bytes. The only thing that can move a span across
319	    documents — and it moves exactly one span.
320	
321	    Every field is a hash, an offset, a verbatim string, or a decision derived from bytes. There is no
322	    field for a repository's version label, and that absence is the design.
323	    """
324	    id: str
325	    source_manifestation_id: str
326	    source_raw_hash: str          # sha256 of the source manifestation's text (its content_hash)
327	    source_start: int
328	    source_end: int
329	    source_span: str              # VERBATIM, as sliced
330	
331	    target_manifestation_id: str
332	    target_raw_hash: str
333	    target_start: int
334	    target_end: int
335	    target_span: str              # VERBATIM, as sliced
336	
337	    canonicalization: str         # ALGORITHM:VERSION under which the two were compared
338	    canonical_span_hash: str      # the exact hash of the shared canonical text
339	
340	    source_identity: str          # the SEMANTIC-BINDING verdict for the SOURCE manifestation
341	    target_identity: str          # ...and for the TARGET. Both must be in IDENTITY_PROVEN.
342	    basis: str                    # how this correspondence was established. Never empty.

345	def make_correspondence(g: 'Graph', source_mid: str, source_start: int, source_end: int,
346	                        target_mid: str, target_start: int, target_end: int,
347	                        basis: str) -> SpanCorrespondence:
348	    """Build a correspondence FROM THE BYTES. It is then still VERIFIED before it grants anything —
349	    the constructor is a convenience, never an authority.

352	    tgt = g.manifestations[target_mid]

357	        .encode()).hexdigest()[:16]

359	        id=f'corr:{cid}',

361	        source_start=source_start, source_end=source_end, source_span=s_span,
362	        target_manifestation_id=target_mid, target_raw_hash=tgt.content_hash,
363	        target_start=target_start, target_end=target_end, target_span=t_span,

367	        # Work RIGHT NOW — never the legacy whole-body `identity.verdict`, which "confirms" any author it
368	        # can find in the references. `verify_correspondence()` re-derives it again and refuses a stale
369	        # stored value, so this is a convenience snapshot, never an authority.
370	        source_identity=g._live_semantic_binding(src),
371	        target_identity=g._live_semantic_binding(tgt),
372	        basis=basis)

969	class Graph:
970	    works: dict[str, Work] = field(default_factory=dict)

977	    def add_edge(self, src: str, dst: str, type: str, status: str, basis: str) -> Edge:
978	        if type not in EDGE_TYPES:
979	            raise ValueError(f'unknown edge type {type!r}')
980	        if status not in ('PROPOSED', 'ASSERTED'):
981	            raise ValueError(f'edge status must be PROPOSED or ASSERTED, not {status!r}')
982	        if not basis or not basis.strip():
983	            # An edge with no evidence is the entire failure we are here to end.
984	            raise ValueError('an edge MUST carry a basis naming the evidence for it')
985	        if status == 'ASSERTED' and type in SPAN_PRESERVING:
986	            # Enforced in the constructor so no caller can talk its way past it, and re-enforced in
987	            # from_json() so no FILE can either.
988	            why = authentication_failure(basis)
989	            if why:
990	                raise ValueError(f'cannot ASSERT {type} — {why}')
991	            why = self.exact_copy_failure(src, dst)
992	            if why:
993	                raise ValueError(f'cannot ASSERT {type} — {why}')
994	        e = Edge(src, dst, type, status, basis)
995	        self.edges.append(e)
996	        return e

998	    def _live_semantic_binding(self, m: 'Manifestation') -> str:
999	        """Re-derive THIS manifestation's semantic binding FROM ITS BYTES and its Work, right now.
1000	
1001	        Correspondence and whole-copy authorization may not trust any STORED identity token — not the
1002	        legacy whole-body `identity.verdict` (which "confirms" any requested author it finds anywhere,
1003	        references included), and not even the profile's cached `semantic_binding`, which a JSON file can
1004	        carry. We run the ONE reducer (event_ledger.derive_binding_core) over the live bytes, exactly as
1005	        the strict loader and the resolver's identity gate do, so a correspondence built when identity
1006	        looked proven is re-judged against what these bytes derive TODAY. A manifestation whose Work we no
1007	        longer hold cannot be identity-derived at all, so it fails closed to UNRESOLVED_BINDING — which is
1008	        not in IDENTITY_PROVEN and therefore authorizes nothing.
1009	        """
1010	        from event_ledger import (observe_text as _obs, derive_binding_core as _bind,  # noqa: PLC0415
1011	                                   UNRESOLVED as _UNR)
1012	        wk = self.works.get(m.work_id)
1013	        if wk is None:
1014	            return _UNR
1015	        sig = _obs(m.text or '')
1016	        live, _basis = _bind(requested_doi=wk.doi or '', requested_title=wk.title or '',
1017	                             requested_authors=list(wk.authors or []), prof=sig,
1018	                             work_kind=wk.kind or 'study')
1019	        return live
